content.problemStatement:
There are NNN islands, numbered 111 to NNN.

Initially, every pair of islands is connected by a bridge. Hence, there are a total of N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)2\frac{N \cdot (N - 1)}{2}2N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)‚Äč bridges. The importance of the ithi^{th}ith island is AiA_iAi‚Äč.

Everule lives on island 111 and enjoys visiting the other islands using bridges. The cost of destroying the bridge between island iii and jjj is Ai‚čÖAjA_i \cdot A_jAi‚Äč‚čÖAj‚Äč.

Dominater has the power to destroy any number of bridges such that the **sum of costs** of destroying the bridges is **at most** CCC.

Find the **minimum** number of islands (including island 111) that Everule can visit if Dominater destroys bridges optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and CCC ‚ÄĒ the number of islands and upper bound on the total cost of destroying bridges.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, denoting the importance of each island.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of islands (including island 111)  Everule can visit.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The array AAA representing the importances of the cities is [2‚čÖ106,4‚čÖ106,5‚čÖ106][2 \cdot 10^6, 4 \cdot 10^6, 5 \cdot 10^6][2‚čÖ106,4‚čÖ106,5‚čÖ106] and the value of CCC is 101810^{18}1018.

The cost of destroying bridge between island 111 and 222 is A1‚čÖA2=8‚čÖ1012A_1 \cdot A_2 = 8 \cdot 10^{12}A1‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč=8‚čÖ1012
The cost of destroying bridge between island 222 and 333 is 20‚čÖ101220 \cdot 10^{12}20‚čÖ1012
The cost of destroying bridge between island 111 and 333 is 10‚čÖ101210 \cdot 10^{12}10‚čÖ1012

The total sum of costs of destroying all the bridges is only 38‚čÖ101238 \cdot 10^{12}38‚čÖ1012, and since C=1018C = 10^{18}C=1018, we can destroy all the bridges as their cost doesn't exceed CCC. This makes Everule unable to visit any island other than 111.
Test Case 2 : Destroy the following bridges :

Islands 111 and 222 : cost is 100100100
Islands 222 and 333 : cost is 111
Islands 222 and 444 : cost is 111
Islands 333 and 444 : cost is 111.

The total cost is 103103103 which is less than C=150C = 150C=150. Now, we can visit only islands 111, 333 and 444 from island 111, since the bridges between 111 and 333, and 111 and 444 have not been destroyed yet. Thus, the answer is 333.
